title: Make button 5 a hard disable for player gaze and focus behavior
summary: 0.6.32 removes the extra grab-navigation handoff logic added in 0.6.30 and 0.6.31. While button 5 is held, the player now fully disables its own gaze and focus behavior and gets out of the way instead of trying to manage a special navigation state.
changes: Detect the grab-navigation override before any gaze or focus resolution work runs.
If button 5 is held, fully release player focus and capture if owned, or stay idle if not owned.
Stop maintaining the special grab-navigation handoff state and snapshot juggling introduced by the previous two slices.
Leave the rest of the player behavior unchanged.
reasoning: The previous handoff slices still felt like they were partially capturing control, which means they were still too opinionated.
The simplest rule is the safest one here: if button 5 is pressed, the player should not do gaze or focus behavior at all.
This keeps the fix inside the same input seam and avoids layering new logic on top of a seam that was already becoming too clever.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: While button 5 is held, the player should completely stop trying to claim gaze or input focus.
VaM navigation behavior should be free to do its own thing while the override is held, without the player fighting it.
knownIssues: This slice does not add the queued post-navigation deadband yet.
This slice does not change slideshow, A-B policy, volume gestures, or spatial audio.
